d and wireless networks will be offline during the early morning on Monday, May 13th. We expect the network to be unavailable beginning at 5AM, and all connectivity should be restored by 10AM at the latest. Update 5/13: The electrical work was postpo

atest. Update 5/13: The electrical work was postponed; all networks are online. We have had reports of some undelivered mail sent via mailing lists on lists.eecs.berkeley.edu. Upon investigating, Google / bMail is refusing to accept some email sent f

le / bMail is refusing to accept some email sent from lists.eecs.berkeley.edu, beginning early on April 24th . We believe this is due to some changes made at the campus level intended to improve mail reliability and reduce spam. We are working with t

reliability and reduce spam. We are working with the people running this project to try to determine the cause of the problem and arrive at a resolution. If you believe you have been impacted by this, any reports of definitely missed emails would be

any reports of definitely missed emails would be helpful to us in assessing the scope of this problem. In the meantime, we suggest that any important or time critical emails be also sent directly to individuals instead of relying entirely on mailing

individuals instead of relying entirely on mailing lists. For important emails that have been sent already, you may wish to follow up with a phone call or email sent directly to their bMail account. This article will be updated as we learn more. Upda

his article will be updated as we learn more. Update April 29, 4pm: This problems seems to have resolved itself. There was no definitive diagnosis unfortunately. We will continue to monitor delivery rates on select mailing lists. The IRIS LDAP server

ates on select mailing lists. The IRIS LDAP servers (ldap.eecs.berkeley.edu) provide usernames and passwords for many EECS applications and servers. On Monday, April 29th at 6pm, we will be upgrading these servers and their related load balancers to

these servers and their related load balancers to Red Hat 8. We plan to gracefully fail the old servers over to the new with no loss of service. As part of this upgrade, new SSL certificates have also been installed. Those accessing our servers pro

so been installed. Those accessing our servers programmatically might notice the certificate upgrade, but most users should not see any changes. Update April 29 6:20pm: The server switchover has been completed. The only issue so far was with a serve

completed. The only issue so far was with a server that was trying to use an older security protocol, TLS 1.1. The new servers require a minimum of TLS 1.2. Please check this setting if you are having problems. Due to planned electrical work, all Co

g problems. Due to planned electrical work, all Cory Hall third floor wired and wireless networks will be offline during the early morning on Monday, April 29th. We expect the network to be unavailable beginning at 5AM, and all connectivity should be

e beginning at 5AM, and all connectivity should be restored by 10AM at the latest. Update 9:06: All networks were back online prior to 9AM. Please contact us at help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you notice any ongoing issues. Due to planned electrical work,

y ongoing issues. Due to planned electrical work, all Cory Hall third floor wired and wireless networks will be offline during the early morning tomorrow, April 25th. We expect the network to be unavailable beginning at 5AM, and all connectivity shou

ilable beginning at 5AM, and all connectivity should be restored by 10AM at the latest. We apologize for the short notice; the need for this shutdown was only identified this afternoon. Please contact us at help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you have any ques

us at help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you have any questions or concerns. [Read more…] about Cory 3F Networks Offline, Morning of Thurs. 4/25 Microsoft will release their patches for the month on Tuesday, April 9th, 2024. Various IRIS Windows and Linux se

April 9th, 2024. Various IRIS Windows and Linux servers will be patched and rebooted Tuesday or Wednesday. PRINT, WINTERM and WDS will automatically patch and reboot Wednesday, April 10th, at 3:00am. WINTERM may be unavailable until 10:00am Wednesday

WINTERM may be unavailable until 10:00am Wednesday, depending on additional software updates. Linux servers lists.eecs, iris2.eecs (for access to the network and roster databases), and login.eecs will be patched and rebooted Tuesday night. DNS server

be patched and rebooted Tuesday night. DNS servers cronus and rhea may also be briefly unavailable while rebooting at approximately 8:00pm Tuesday. On Wednesday, April 10th, the departmental DHCP server is scheduled for downtime from 10pm to 10:30pm

ver is scheduled for downtime from 10pm to 10:30pm (actual downtime is expected to be shorter than scheduled). [Read more…] about April IRIS Server Patching We are experiencing a variety of issues with the EECS border firewall, where connections to p

h the EECS border firewall, where connections to ports which should be open are being blocked. We are currently investigating. [Read more…] about EECS Firewall Issues, April 4th For many years the Department has operated an anonymous ftp server at ft

artment has operated an anonymous ftp server at ftp.eecs.berkeley.edu and ftp.cs.berkeley.edu. Before web sites, this was how software and papers were distributed to the public. Since web sites are the preferred way to distribute digital assets thes

he preferred way to distribute digital assets these days, no new content has been published on our ftp servers for over a decade. We will be ending support for anonymous ftp access effective May 1, 2024. After that time, the current contents of our

024. After that time, the current contents of our ftp archive will continue to be made available via a web page at https://ftp.eecs.berkeley.edu and https://ftp.cs.berkeley.edu/. Update: May 1, 1pm: The ftp server has been shut down. The replacement

he ftp server has been shut down. The replacement web page is up at https://ftp.eecs.berkeley.edu We are scheduling a NetApp ONTAP upgrade, to update our NetApp fileserver, from 8am to 12pm on Saturday, March 30, 2024 . All Home directories and Proje

y, March 30, 2024 . All Home directories and Project shares (CIFS/SMB and NFS) may experience disconnection during the update that may cause data loss on active, open files. Please close your open files and save them before Saturday Morning. Service

les and save them before Saturday Morning. Services (e.g. webservice) that depend on this storage will also be temporarily down or unresponsive. When maintenance is completed, NFS clients should automatically resume normal operation, but a reboot sho

atically resume normal operation, but a reboot should help if they don’t. CIFS clients (such as Windows and MacOS) may need to remount filesystems, or simply be rebooted. [Read more…] about NetApp Fileserver Maintenance, Saturday March 30, 2024 Betwe

eserver Maintenance, Saturday March 30, 2024 Between 11AM and 5PM on Friday, March 29th, we will be replacing the access switches which provide wired network connectivity on the 1st floor of Cory Hall (including the mezzanine) and the 2nd floor of So

(including the mezzanine) and the 2nd floor of Soda Hall. Wired networks in these locations will experience various interruptions during this time. Most of these interruptions will be brief (<30 seconds), but some cables on Cory 1F/1M will be down f

nds), but some cables on Cory 1F/1M will be down for an extended period of time (over an hour). Wireless networks will not be affected. Primary networking in the server rooms (165 Cory, 287-290 Soda) will not be affected by this work. The exception i

will not be affected by this work. The exception is the floor boxes in those rooms, which will be affected. Update 3/29 15:41: This maintenance has been completed and all networks are back online. Please email help@eecs.berkeley.edu if you notice any
